hard_constraints.continuity_rule: exact same mechanism state before resume
hard_constraints.search_scope: zquad family only around y10_x2 and y8_x0

current_faithful_best_markov_row.rationale: Reference overall-best faithful12-base zquad candidate from the previous pass. Kept in-batch as the main mean anchor.

current_faithful_max_markov_row.rationale: Reference max-best faithful12-base zquad sibling from the previous pass. Kept in-batch as the Ka2_y/max anchor.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zquad_y10_x2
candidate_specs.rationale: Reference overall-best faithful12-base zquad candidate from the previous pass. Kept in-batch as the main mean anchor.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zquad_y8_x0
candidate_specs.rationale: Reference max-best faithful12-base zquad sibling from the previous pass. Kept in-batch as the Ka2_y/max anchor.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zquad_y9_x0
candidate_specs.rationale: Direct midpoint between the max-oriented y8_x0 and the stronger-mean y10_x0 neighborhood. Tests whether one extra second of ±Y dwell is enough to recover mean without giving back the max benefit.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zquad_y8_x1
candidate_specs.rationale: Add only a minimal 1 s intermediate ±X buffer to the max-oriented y8_x0 motif, to see whether mean can recover while keeping the Ka2_y ceiling near the current best.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zquad_y9_x1
candidate_specs.rationale: Symmetric midpoint interpolation around the two best anchors: slightly lighter ±Y than y10_x2, but keep a minimal 1 s ±X buffer.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zquad_y10_x1
candidate_specs.rationale: Trim the current mean-optimal x-buffer from 2 s to 1 s while preserving the stronger y=10 exposure. Intended to lower Ka2_y/max with minimal damage to the mean.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_d9_then_zquad_y10_x2
candidate_specs.rationale: Very mild dwell rebalance: shorten the preceding xpair outer holds from 10 s to 9 s while keeping the current overall-best zquad block unchanged. Tests whether slightly less pre-zquad x loading helps Ka2_y/max.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_d11_then_zquad_y8_x0
candidate_specs.rationale: Mirror mild rebalance on the max branch: lengthen the preceding xpair outer holds from 10 s to 11 s while keeping the max-oriented zquad_y8_x0 block unchanged. Tests whether stronger pre-zquad outer-state protection can shave Ka2_y/max further.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zquad_y10_x0back2
candidate_specs.rationale: Mild order/balance tweak at fixed zquad family: keep total 2 s of intermediate ±X dwell, but place it only on the closing +X state rather than splitting it symmetrically. This explicitly tries to protect the already-good resume channels near node 12.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zquad_ypos10_x1_yneg8_x1
candidate_specs.rationale: One closely related three-level Y-exposure tweak integrated into the zquad family: preserve the zero-level ±X buffers, keep the positive-Y hold at 10 s, but lighten the negative-Y hold to 8 s. This tests whether a slightly softer return-side Y exposure can lower Ka2_y/max while holding most of the mean gain.

bottom_line.ceiling_verdict: not materially: the follow-up only moved max by 0.001 points against the current max-best branch (and 0.030 against the current overall-best), far too small to count as a real Ka2_y ceiling repair.

scientific_conclusion: The focused zquad follow-up did not beat the current faithful12-base overall-best mean anchor (late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zquad_ypos10_x1_yneg8_x1 is still weaker on mean), and it also failed to lower the Ka2_y/max ceiling materially. This suggests the present late11 zquad family is already near its local limit under the real dual-axis continuity rule.
